question_structure.context: As shown in Figure A, two identical wooden blocks A and B, each with a mass of 4 kg, are stacked on a horizontal table. Under the action of a horizontal force $F_{1}$ of 16 N, blocks A and B move together in uniform linear motion. ($g$ is taken as $10 \, \mathrm{N/kg}$)
question_structure.sub_question_1: At this moment, what is the magnitude of the frictional force $F_{f1}$ on the upper surface of block B? What is the magnitude of the frictional force $F_{f2}$ on the lower surface of block B?
question_structure.sub_question_2: What is the coefficient of kinetic friction between the tabletop and the wooden block?
question_structure.sub_question_3: If A and B are placed closely together on a horizontal tabletop (as shown in Figure B) and a horizontal force $F_{2}$ is applied to A to make them move together at a constant velocity, what is the magnitude of the elastic force exerted by A on B?

sub_question_1.step_1: A is in uniform motion, and there is no force acting on it in the horizontal direction, so the frictional force on the upper surface of B is $F_{f1}=0$.

sub_question_2.step_5: Combine step_3 and step_4, and solve to obtain $\mu=0.2$

sub_question_3.step_6: When A and B are placed tightly together on a horizontal table, $F_{NB}=m_{B}g$. Do not add any other content.
sub_question_3.step_7: The friction force on B is $F_{fB}=\mu F_{NB}=8N$.
sub_question_3.step_8: By simultaneously solving step_6 and step_7, we obtain: $F_{B}=F_{fB}=8N$

image_captions: The image shows two physics diagrams, labeled A and B. In diagram A, two blocks labeled A and B are stacked vertically, with block B on the bottom and resting on a surface. A horizontal force F1 is applied to block B. In diagram B, blocks A and B are positioned horizontally side-by-side, both resting on a surface. A horizontal force F2 is applied to block A. The surfaces are all indicated with a horizontal lined pattern.

step_1.physical_theorem: Newton's First Law (Inertia) and Equilibrium of forces

result_quantity.name: The frictional force on the upper surface of block B
The frictional force on the lower surface of block B
Total Normal force on the table
Frictional force on the lower surface of block B
Coefficient of kinetic friction between the tabletop and the wooden block
The magnitude of the elastic force exerted by A on B
